rationale: The cited paper is a strong conceptual predecessor: it shows incidental prices anchor willingness to pay in field/lab/auction settings, while the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending reference-price and price-perception work on spending decisions. Given this clear topical/mechanistic overlap (price cues shaping valuation and spending), the citation could plausibly be a theory or mechanism source rather than a mere background mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no local citation context, it is still not safe to drop.
